heroHeadline: Urumqi Non-Escalation Holds Day 11; US-Iran Crisis Delays Second Af-Pak Round
heroSubtitle: The Af-Pak Urumqi non-escalation framework has held for 11 consecutive days — the longest lull since Op Ghazab-il-Haq began Feb 26. However, Pakistan's diplomatic bandwidth is almost entirely consumed by the US-Iran ceasefire crisis: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz again Apr 19, the US delegation arrived in Islamabad Apr 20, and Iran publicly declined to join talks as the ceasefire approached its April 22 expiry. The second Urumqi round — originally due 'end of April' — is quietly slipping into May.
footerNote: Data sourced from UNAMA, SIGAR, ACLED, Brown University Costs of War Project, US DoD, Reuters, BBC, Tolo News, Dawn, Al Jazeera, HRW, OHCHR
